gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Build the full `docs/lib-index/` report by walking every phylum directory under `lib/`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Propagates the first filesystem error (with its path implied by the walk) — never a silent"] # [doc = " skip of a present source."] pub fn build_lib_index (repo_root : & Path) -> std :: io :: Result < LibIndexReport > { let mut items = Vec :: new () ; let mut flagged = Vec :: new () ; for (phylum , dir) in phylum_roots (repo_root) ? { for path in collect_myc (& dir) ? { let rel = repo_rel (repo_root , & path) ; let src = std :: fs :: read_to_string (& path) ? ; index_file (& phylum , & rel , & src , & mut items , & mut flagged) ; } } items . sort_by (| a , b | { (& a . phylum , & a . nodule , a . line , & a . symbol) . cmp (& (& b . phylum , & b . nodule , b . line , & b . symbol)) }) ; flagged . sort_by (| a , b | a . item . cmp (& b . item) . then (a . reason . cmp (& b . reason))) ; Ok (LibIndexReport { items , flagged }) }

") ; out . push_str (& format ! ("> **Honesty:** `{HONESTY_TAG}`
")) ; out . push_str ("> Use the index to find where to `Read`, not as an authoritative reference.

("| {symbol} | {} | {file_line} | {sig} | {summary} | {} |
" , item . kind , item . tag)) ; } out . push ('
') ; } } out . push_str ("## Flagged items

") ; out . push_str ("Constructs/gaps the heuristic could not (or does not yet) extract (G2: never silently \

" ,) ; if report . flagged . is_empty () { out . push_str ("*(none)*

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Escape `|`/`*` for a cell rendered as **plain prose** (summary text, a flagged-item reason) —"] # [doc = " content that is NOT wrapped in a code span. `.myc` doc-comments are copied verbatim from source"] # [doc = " (never invented, G2) and occasionally contain literal math notation like `3^m * carry`;"] # [doc = " unescaped, a linter reads `* c` as emphasis markup (M-1004's `MD037` finding). Deliberately does"] # [doc = " **not** escape `_` — CommonMark/GFM already suppresses intraword `_` emphasis (`snake_case_name`"] # [doc = " never renders as emphasis), and identifiers with underscores are extremely common in this"] # [doc = " prose; escaping them would insert a literal backslash that breaks a plain-text grep for the"] # [doc = " identifier — the opposite of this index's grep-friendly purpose. `index.json` is unaffected"] # [doc = " (JSON, not Markdown — no escaping needed there)."] pub (crate) fn md_escape_prose (s : & str) -> String { s . replace ('|' , "\\|") . replace ('*' , "\\*") }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Discover phylum roots under `lib/` (sorted, deterministic): every immediate subdirectory. A new"] # [doc = " phylum directory is picked up automatically — the extractor never needs to be told about it."] pub (crate) fn phylum_roots (repo_root : & Path) -> std :: io :: Result < Vec < (String , PathBuf) > > { let lib = repo_root . join (LIB_DIR) ; let mut out = Vec :: new () ; if ! lib . exists () { return Ok (out) ; } let mut dirs : Vec < PathBuf > = std :: fs :: read_dir (& lib) ? . filter_map (std :: result :: Result :: ok) . map (| e | e . path ()) . filter (| p | p . is_dir ()) . collect () ; dirs . sort () ; for dir in dirs { let Some (name) = dir . file_name () . map (| n | n . to_string_lossy () . into_owned ()) else { continue ; } ; out . push ((name , dir)) ; } Ok (out) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Recursively collect `.myc` files under `root`, sorted for determinism."] pub (crate) fn collect_myc (root : & Path) -> std :: io :: Result < Vec < PathBuf > > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; let mut stack = vec ! [root . to_path_buf ()] ; while let Some (dir) = stack . pop () { for entry in std :: fs :: read_dir (& dir) ? { let entry = entry ? ; let path = entry . path () ; if path . is_dir () { stack . push (path) ; } else if path . extension () . and_then (| e | e . to_str ()) == Some ("myc") { out . push (path) ; } } } out . sort () ; Ok (out) }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Index one `.myc` file: the nodule marker, every `fn`, every `type` + its constructors, and any"] # [doc = " unrecognised top-level construct (flagged, never dropped)."] pub (crate) fn index_file (phylum : & str , rel : & str , src : & str , items : & mut Vec < LibIndexItem > , flagged : & mut Vec < Flagged > ,) { let nodule = nodule_name (src) . unwrap_or_else (| | path_stem (rel)) ; if nodule_name (src) . is_none () { flagged . push (Flagged { item : rel . to_owned () , reason : "no `// nodule:` marker / `nodule X.Y;` declaration found — grouped by \
                filename instead" . to_owned () , }) ; } let summary = full_summary (src) ; if let Err (e) = mycelium_proj :: parse_header (src) { flagged . push (Flagged { item : format ! ("{nodule} (header)") , reason : format ! ("header metadata parse error at line {}: {} — @summary/@version/etc. may be \
                 unavailable; fn/type extraction still ran" , e . line , e . message) , }) ; } items . push (LibIndexItem { symbol : nodule . clone () , kind : "nodule" . to_owned () , phylum : phylum . to_owned () , nodule : nodule . clone () , file : rel . to_owned () , line : nodule_decl_line (src) , signature : Some (format ! ("nodule {nodule}")) , summary , tag : ITEM_TAG . to_owned () , }) ; let lines : Vec < & str > = src . lines () . collect () ; for (sig , line) in fn_signatures (src) { let name = fn_name (& sig) . unwrap_or_else (| | "fn" . to_owned ()) ; items . push (LibIndexItem { symbol : format ! ("{nodule}::{name}") , kind : "fn" . to_owned () , phylum : phylum . to_owned () , nodule : nodule . clone () , file : rel . to_owned () , line , signature : Some (sig) , summary : preceding_doc (& lines , line) , tag : ITEM_TAG . to_owned () , }) ; } let (decls , problems) = type_declarations (src) ; for decl in decls { items . push (LibIndexItem { symbol : format ! ("{nodule}::{}" , decl . name) , kind : "type" . to_owned () , phylum : phylum . to_owned () , nodule : nodule . clone () , file : rel . to_owned () , line : decl . start_line , signature : Some (decl . text . clone ()) , summary : preceding_doc (& lines , decl . start_line) , tag : ITEM_TAG . to_owned () , }) ; for (ctor_name , ctor_text , ctor_line) in & decl . ctors { items . push (LibIndexItem { symbol : format ! ("{nodule}::{}::{ctor_name}" , decl . name) , kind : "ctor" . to_owned () , phylum : phylum . to_owned () , nodule : nodule . clone () , file : rel . to_owned () , line : * ctor_line , signature : Some (ctor_text . clone ()) , summary : None , tag : ITEM_TAG . to_owned () , }) ; } } for (name , line , reason) in problems { flagged . push (Flagged { item : format ! ("{nodule}::{name}") , reason : format ! ("{reason} (starting line {line})") , }) ; } for (kw , line) in unrecognized_top_level (src) { flagged . push (Flagged { item : format ! ("{nodule} (line {line})") , reason : format ! ("unextracted top-level construct `{kw}` at line {line} — only nodule/fn/type are \
                 indexed today; not silently dropped, just out of scope (no `lib/` nodule uses \
                 this DN-26 `Item` form yet)") , }) ; } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The 1-based line of the file's nodule declaration (the `nodule X.Y;` statement if present, else"] # [doc = " the `// nodule:` marker comment, else `1` as an honest last resort). Mirrors `apiref::nodule_name`'s"] # [doc = " two-pass scan so the reported line matches the name it found."] pub (crate) fn nodule_decl_line (src : & str) -> u32 { for (i , line) in src . lines () . enumerate () { if line . trim () . starts_with ("nodule ") { return (i + 1) as u32 ; } } for (i , line) in src . lines () . enumerate () { if line . trim () . starts_with ("// nodule:") { return (i + 1) as u32 ; } } 1 }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The full (possibly multi-line) `@summary` text: the structured header's first line plus any"] # [doc = " contiguous, non-`@`, non-blank `//` continuation lines immediately following it."] # [doc = " `mycelium_proj::parse_header`'s own scan stops at the first such line (it only consumes"] # [doc = " contiguous `// @key: value` lines), so a multi-line summary — common in `lib/` headers, e.g."] # [doc = " `lib/compiler/ambient.myc`'s four-line `@summary` — is otherwise silently truncated to its"] # [doc = " first line. Mirrors `apiref::preceding_doc`'s backward joiner, scanning forward instead — a"] # [doc = " deliberate, documented divergence from `apiref.rs` (its consumer only ever uses the first-line"] # [doc = " summary, so it never needed this). Verbatim from source, never invented (G2); `None` when there"] # [doc = " is no `@summary` line at all."] pub (crate) fn full_summary (src : & str) -> Option < String > { let lines : Vec < & str > = src . lines () . collect () ; let start = lines . iter () . position (| line | { line . trim () . strip_prefix ("//") . map (str :: trim) . is_some_and (| c | c . starts_with ("@summary:")) }) ? ; let first = lines [start] . trim () . strip_prefix ("//") ? . trim () . strip_prefix ("@summary:") ? . trim () . to_owned () ; let mut parts = vec ! [first] ; for line in & lines [start + 1 ..] { let t = line . trim () ; let Some (rest) = t . strip_prefix ("//") else { break ; } ; let rest = rest . trim () ; if rest . is_empty () || rest . starts_with ('@') || rest . starts_with ("nodule") { break ; } parts . push (rest . to_owned ()) ; } let joined = parts . join (" ") ; (! joined . is_empty ()) . then_some (joined) }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Extract every top-level `type NAME = ...;` declaration. Terminated by the first line (from the"] # [doc = " `type` line onward) whose trimmed text ends with `;` — Mycelium constructor argument lists"] # [doc = " don't themselves contain `;`, so this is unambiguous over every real `lib/` file (checked by"] # [doc = " hand across `lib/std/` + `lib/compiler/`). Returns the parsed declarations plus a"] # [doc = " `(name, start_line, reason)` list for any declaration the heuristic could not close (never"] # [doc = " silently dropped — G2; the caller turns these into `Flagged` entries)."] pub (crate) fn type_declarations (src : & str) -> (Vec < TypeDecl > , Vec < (String , u32 , String) >) { let lines : Vec < & str > = src . lines () . collect () ; let mut out = Vec :: new () ; let mut problems = Vec :: new () ; let mut i = 0 ; while i < lines . len () { let t = lines [i] . trim () ; let Some (after_kw) = t . strip_prefix ("type ") else { i += 1 ; continue ; } ; let start_line = (i + 1) as u32 ; let name : String = after_kw . trim_start () . chars () . take_while (| c | ! matches ! (c , '=' | '[') && ! c . is_whitespace ()) . collect () ; let mut block : Vec < (& str , u32) > = vec ! [(lines [i] , start_line)] ; let mut j = i ; let mut closed = lines [i] . trim_end () . ends_with (';') ; while ! closed && j + 1 < lines . len () { j += 1 ; block . push ((lines [j] , (j + 1) as u32)) ; closed = lines [j] . trim_end () . ends_with (';') ; } if ! closed { problems . push ((name , start_line , "type declaration never reached a terminating `;` before EOF — not indexed" . to_owned () ,)) ; i += 1 ; continue ; } let joined : String = block . iter () . map (| (l , _) | l . trim ()) . filter (| l | ! l . starts_with ("//")) . collect :: < Vec < _ > > () . join (" ") ; let text = joined . trim_end_matches (';') . trim () . to_owned () ; let body_after_eq = joined . split_once ('=') . map_or (joined . as_str () , | (_ , b) | b) ; let body = body_after_eq . trim () . trim_end_matches (';') . trim () ; let ctors = split_top_level_ctors (body , & block) ; out . push (TypeDecl { name , start_line , text , ctors , }) ; i = j + 1 ; } (out , problems) }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Split a type body into top-level (`|`-separated, depth-0 over `()[]{}`) constructor texts, then"] # [doc = " best-effort attribute each to the source line it appears on."] pub (crate) fn split_top_level_ctors (body : & str , block : & [(& str , u32)] ,) -> Vec < (String , String , u32) > { let mut ctors : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; let mut depth : i32 = 0 ; let mut cur = String :: new () ; for ch in body . chars () { match ch { '(' | '[' | '{' => { depth += 1 ; cur . push (ch) ; } ')' | ']' | '}' => { depth -= 1 ; cur . push (ch) ; } '|' if depth == 0 => { ctors . push (std :: mem :: take (& mut cur) . trim () . to_owned ()) ; } _ => cur . push (ch) , } } if ! cur . trim () . is_empty () { ctors . push (cur . trim () . to_owned ()) ; } let fallback_line = block . first () . map_or (0 , | (_ , l) | * l) ; ctors . into_iter () . filter (| c | ! c . is_empty ()) . map (| text | { let name : String = text . chars () . take_while (| c | c . is_alphanumeric () || * c == '_') . collect () ; let line = attribute_line (block , & name) . unwrap_or (fallback_line) ; (name , text , line) }) . collect () }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Find the line in `block` whose trimmed text contains `ident` as a whole word (bounded by"] # [doc = " non-identifier characters on both sides) — a best-effort constructor→line attribution. `None`"] # [doc = " when no line matches (the caller falls back to the type's start line): a heuristic-on-heuristic,"] # [doc = " deliberately not claimed more precise than that."] pub (crate) fn attribute_line (block : & [(& str , u32)] , ident : & str) -> Option < u32 > { if ident . is_empty () { return None ; } for (raw , line) in block { let t = raw . trim () ; let bytes = t . as_bytes () ; let mut search_from = 0 ; while let Some (pos) = t [search_from ..] . find (ident) { let abs = search_from + pos ; let before_ok = abs == 0 || ! is_ident_byte (bytes [abs - 1]) ; let after = abs + ident . len () ; let after_ok = after >= bytes . len () || ! is_ident_byte (bytes [after]) ; if before_ok && after_ok { return Some (* line) ; } search_from = abs + ident . len () ; if search_from >= t . len () { break ; } } } None }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: pub (crate) fn is_ident_byte (b : u8) -> bool { b . is_ascii_alphanumeric () || b == b'_' }
gaps.reason: method call `.is_ascii_alphanumeric(...)` has no proven-emitted free-fn referent in this file (neither a D4-mangled inherent `Type__is_ascii_alphanumeric` nor a bare inherent recorded by this file's left-to-right pass, nor a prim_map / combinator row). Emitting bare `is_ascii_alphanumeric(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Keywords for DN-26 `Item` constructs this extractor does not (yet) parse: `use` (cross-nodule"] # [doc = " imports/re-exports), `trait`, `impl`, `object`, `derive`, `lower`, `default`. None appear in"] # [doc = " `lib/` today, but a future nodule using one must be FLAGGED, never silently absent (G2)."] const UNRECOGNIZED_KEYWORDS : & [& str] = & ["use " , "trait " , "impl " , "object " , "derive " , "lower " , "default " ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const UNRECOGNIZED_KEYWORDS` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Top-level (column-0) lines starting with an unrecognised `Item` keyword."] pub (crate) fn unrecognized_top_level (src : & str) -> Vec < (& 'static str , u32) > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; for (i , line) in src . lines () . enumerate () { if line . starts_with (| c : char | c . is_whitespace ()) || line . trim () . is_empty () { continue ; } for kw in UNRECOGNIZED_KEYWORDS . iter () . copied () { if line . starts_with (kw) { out . push ((kw . trim () , (i + 1) as u32)) ; break ; } } } out }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)
